Hi Steph

We want to ensure we are providing high-quality, engaging sessions for your center. Our goal is to continue doing what works well and adapt any elements that may not be meeting your needs to better suit your center when delivering Mini Monstars sessions.

Hi Steph,

It was so nice speaking with you today, and I am thrilled you enjoyed your first session enough to lock in for the year.

I am happy to confirm that we have increased your session time to 50 minutes at a cost of $210 + GST per session. This will include 20 minutes for Junior Kindy and 30 minutes for Kindy. Please note that sessions will continue through school holidays.

I am also pleased to confirm you are all set with Coach Hannah, continuing fortnightly from your original start date. We are so glad to have you with us!
As a friendly reminder, we maintain a four-session notice policy for any cancellations.

I will follow up with you after your first session to gather your feedback, but please feel free to reach out to us at any time prior if you have questions.


Kind Regards,

We are incredibly excited to be running our program within your centre.

I’m excited to share that as we shuffle things for Term 2, the lovely Coach Willow will be joining you! I’ve attached her WWC Blue Card for your records. Please feel free to reach out if you need anything else. I’ll check back soon for your feedback to make sure you’re continuing to  love your Mini Monstar sessions!

Kind Regards
Katie 🙂

Hi Holly,

I hope you’re having a wonderful week!

Following up on our recent messages regarding the schedule change for Eden Academy Park Ridge, I am pleased to let you know that I have updated our system to reflect your request for fortnightly sessions.

We have everything set to begin on Friday, 03 July 2026 . From this date, the sessions will run fortnightly from 10:00 AM to 11:30 AM, led by Coach Emily-Jane Harvey.

We are so glad to hear that the program has been a hit with the children, and we are more than happy to accommodate this new schedule to fit your budget.

Please let me know if you need anything else!

Kind Regards,

Katie 🙂

Hi Holly,

We can absolutely look into that option for you! Just so I can best help with your request, may I ask if the interest in a fortnightly schedule is related to the budget?

Thanks so much
Katie🙂

Hi Chloe,

I hope you’re having a good week and your little chicks are doing well.

I just wanted to follow up and see how the feedback from the gymnastics sessions went? I’m looking forward to hearing any insights or comments that were shared.

Kind Regards
Katie
sent email but feedback on day was they sounded like they LOVED IT!
attended with emily , emily fine to take solo
emily did shadow of gymnastic session and Katie will shadow her first session

Hi Holly,

Thanks for the great news! I’ve confirmed with Emily that we’ll be switching over to gymnastics for Eden Academy Park Ridge starting June 12th.

Your sessions will run
 Weekly 
10:00 AM to 11:30 AM. 
Gymnastics

To help support your large class sizes, both Emily-Jane and I will be there to ensure a smooth and fun transition! I’ve attached my Blue Card below for your records.


We're so excited to get started!

Kind Regards
Katie 🙂

Hi Holly,

It was such a pleasure speaking with you today! As we mentioned, in addition to our music programs, we’d love to help you out with our sports, yoga, and gymnastics incursions too.
https://www.minimonstars.net/
https://www.minimonstars.net/early-years-learning-framework

I know you're considering starting gymnastics earlier than Term 3, so I'll look forward to your update once you've had a chance to chat with Chloe and Demi.

Feel free to give me a call whenever you're ready to touch base. Thanks so much for reaching out, and I look forward to speaking with you again soon!

Kind Regards
Katie 🙂
everything going well, love it need to organize make up for last Friday but said all good as Emily is great and always on top of these these

Hi Clhoe,Dani and Team,
I hope you are all having a great week.
We have a new coach who needs to complete an on-site observation as part of our induction process. This session involves attending a center alongside your regular Head coach to observe the program, assist with setup, and participate in warm-ups to better understand our operational standards and role requirements.
I would like to send Kaitlyn Walsh to shadow your regular coach during their next session. For your records, I have attached her Working With Children Check (WWCC) information to this email. 

Please let me know if this works for your schedule or if you have any questions regarding the induction visit.
Kind RegardsKatie Thomas

Hi Nelly and Team,
As you would have noticed, Dakota has attended the last two sessions with me at your centre. Recently I have taken on more leadership tasks and require more time for them. Therefore, Dakota will run your sessions starting 8th of June. The children enjoyed interacting with Dakota and got to know her a little so the transition to a new coach should be easier for them. 
I will remain your contact for any changes to sessions, times etc. I will also make regular contact to ensure everything is running smoothly with Dakota and the children are enjoying their sessions. 
Additionally, if you have any other questions, contact our state manager Katie - kthomas@minimonstars.com
I'm excited to have Dakota leading these sessions and look forward to this transition. I have enjoyed my time as a coach with your centre and look forward to popping in to see how everyone is going. Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for supporting me with this exciting new opportunity. 
I have included Dakotas Blue card WWC please let me know if you require additional information for your register.
image.png
Warm Regards,
Jess 🌟

timeline.t: Spoke to Emily, she said Hannah has been great, all positive, children love her and follow her instructions. No news regarding HO budget restrictions, she said there centre is doing well so hopefully it won’t affect them.

Spoke to Emily, she said Hannah has been really good, the children are excited to see see her when she comes in, she engages them well. They can tell that Farrah has taught her and they are happy with how is she running the sessions.

Spoke to Sam, one of the educators, the kids had a lot of fun with Farrah as always, she introduced them to rugby and they loved it. Told her that Hannah was on holidays but will be back next week, she said that’s great because the kids miss her

Spoke to Emily, they love Farrah, she said she got some feedback from the educators too so will send me an email. She was busy on the floor

Hannah has been doing well from my observation, she engages with children in a similar manner as Farrah and the children love her too

 

The mini monsters ladies are AMAZING!! They are so kind and very caring towards every child.

They work with a certain child who doesn’t normally like to participate but they really encourage them.  

Hannah did really well today! I think she will be fantastic!

Hi team,Hope you’re well I’ve tried calling a few times today but couldn’t get through, it seems there might be an issue with the phone line.Just wanted to check in and see how the sessions have been going, and if there’s any feedback you’d like to share.Have a lovely day!Viki Hamawi

23/04: Hi Viki,


Thanks for the email.

 

A little update on the centre – we had our wonderful Admin leave the centre so it is now just me in the Admin and Centre Manager roles.

 

If you can’t reach me via phone – email is the next best thing 😊

 

Farrah is wonderful!

Couldn’t speak more highly of her – her interactions with the staff and children are so warm and friendly.

I love that she offered sessions with our nursery room too as we struggle to find incursions for them at times.

 

Also just wanted to mention we do not want soccer included in the program as we already have another provider delivering this program.

 

Thank you!

 

Logo, company name

Description automatically generated

 

Emily Brice

Centre Manager
Spoke to emily, they are very happy with Farrah, she is great with the children and all posotive feedback.

Spoke to Khalee, they are happy with Farrah she is good with the children, she sat with them this morning and waited for them to finish breakfast while talking to them, she involves the babies as well. Apologies again for everything that has happened with Allira and she said they they were greatful for the quick actions/replacement and regular check ins. Asked her to send me feedbacks regardless if its positive or negative

Allira has missed the rescheduled session and didn't advise the centre. Allira was MIA so Farrah ran the session on Thursday and also reschedlued their missed session. Allira sent a message and resigned. Farrah will run the sessions till we find a new coach. Centre was happy with Farrah howver a bit upset with Allira's actions. Hi Kahlee,

Thank you for your time on the phone today.

As I mentioned, Allira is no longer with the company, and I would like to apologise again to you, your team, and the parents for her actions.

I’m really glad to hear that you had a great session with Farrah today, and I’d also like to thank the team and the children for giving her the opportunity. I’m confident she will continue to run the sessions to the high standard we expect from our coaches.

As discussed, Farrah will run a make-up session on Monday 30th of March at 10:00am, and your Thursday sessions will continue as normal.

If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out.

Have a great day,
Viki  Hamawi

Allira didn't attend the session on Thursday, she called the centre but not me. She also marked her attendance so I only found out that she missed the session when I called the centre. Asked her to call them and apologise and try to reschedule.

Spoke to Kahlee( centre admin), she had some feedback from the edicators: gets a bit overwhelming for the younger children as there are too many instructions at once, activities need to be more age appropriate so they can all join in. Will sen d an email with the feedback. Good morning, Viki

Thank you for your call this morning

 

Some of the feedback we have received is

 

. Educators feel that with the under 2 -3 age groups it feels a little overwhelming they often loose interest and play with other equipment and roam around the playground they start following each other, the instruction that are set out for the younger ones don’t seem to be age appropriate and on the day the lady seemed a little frustrated and flustered. 

. The under 2 age group children really like the basketball skills and loved the running with the ladders, but the lady seems o be overwhelmed when the children loose interest would be good if she can give simpler instructions

. The older children loved it some children that hadn’t participated in other weeks got in and gave it a go last week

 

I’m sorry if this wasn’t too descriptive but this is the feedback we have received so far

 

I hope this helps

Have a good day

Thank you

Spoke to Carly, confirmed that we will be running the sessions for the next few weeks till we get a final decision from head office. Sent an email as the director Emily wasn't in.

timeline.t: Visited the centre to drop off Mikey Monstar, spoke to Gheetika, she said they are really enjoying the sessions, lot of positive feedback from parents and extra bookings, might need to adjust time a bit because there are a lot of little ones and they don't get enough turns in the sport activites, if we need to add an extra 30 mins they will let me, happy with Monty's attitude, very postive
No feedback required, spoke to them already on the 17th, very positive feedback of both Mantej and Eddie

Spoke to Geethika, they are very happy with the session, the children are very engaged, Mantej and Eddie are both great, they are excited to continue, she loved how we constantly check in, very happy with the program.

Spoke to Natalie( she is admin I think) she said that the session are really good, had lots of casual bookings, kids seem to enjoy it a lot. They have been using the app, doing the challenges. Very positive feedback.
Spoke to Geetika, they love the session, today's class was outside, they used a lot of equipment, took advantage of the outside area and it was very good. Have a lot of casual bookings.

Visited the centre to set up the app, spoke to the director Geetika, they love the program, posotive feeedback from parents and lots of casual bookins. Kids love both Mantej and Eddie, they are both very good with the children. Make sure we tailor all sessions to babies as well as parents would like to see that they are involved in the program.

Spoke to both Gheetika and Aman, they said the session was a lot better thna last time, they can see the effort Mantej has put in, they all had fun (they had bad weather again) and the session was great
Spoke to Geethika, they loved Edddie, he was very engaging, all the kids wanted to join in, very good feeback from educators as well.

Spoke to Geetika (director)and Aman (edication leader), due to the weather they couldn't run the session outside so they had to be inside where they couldn't really play sports. Mantej did a lot of activities with them, the preschollers and older toddler were really engaged and enjoyed but the younger ones need to be involved a bit more.

timeline.t:
Hi  Alicia

We are so looking forward to welcoming new coaches soon, have others with potential availability coming up and hope to have Brooke back in action shortly. Hi  Monique,

I am very sorry to hear your feedback, as we pride ourselves on the high quality of our programs and their alignment with the ELF.

I appreciate you providing such comprehensive details. To ensure your next session meets your expectations, I will send one of my leaders, Coach Aisa to attend the session with Fanta. They will provide support and ensure the program is being delivered to our required standards, specifically regarding the level of engagement and "upskilling" for the older children and ensure it is meeting your needs.
If there are specific skills you expected the children to learn in these first four weeks, please share them with me. We can then review where those skills fit into the 46 week program and shuffling some more advanced skills forward provided the children are demonstrating the required safety and foundational skills for progression.
I look forward to hearing about the next session .
I have attached Aisa's WWC Blue Card below.
 

Kind Regards,

Katie

GC&E Rochedale South

to me

Hi Katie,

 

Thanks for your email, and I’m pretty sure that I gave an emailed feedback the last time.

 

Offhand, I’m not impressed that the teacher was late again – arrived 9:40am. While I understand that last week she was still looking for the Centre, but today nothing has changed. We try to get the children organised to be ready for the activity before 9:30am, but this does not seem to matter as your teacher is late.

 

I was also expecting an improvement in the activity, and because you said that she will need to build on the skills of the children. The children in Kindergarten 1 did the same activities as with last week, which were much suited for smaller children. These activities were too easy for the older children. I didn’t see any “upskilling” in terms of what the children could do or explore.

 

It is unlikely that we will continue with the program if we do not see an improvement in the content and/or delivery.  I hope there is more to this gymnastics program apart from balancing bean bags on children’s heads.

 

Thanks again for your support.

 

 

Kind regards,

 

Monique Orendain

Centre Manager

patterns.prevention:
• Assign dedicated primary coaches to each centre for minimum 8-week commitment to build consistency and trust with staff.

• Audit all centres with 2+ missed sessions in past 3 months and confirm coach availability before next scheduled visit.

• Create backup coach protocol—identify secondary coach for each centre so cancellations due to single-coach absence drop to zero.

• Send state managers a weekly coverage tracker showing assigned coaches, absences, and at-risk centres by Friday EOD.

patterns.prevention:
• Audit coach engagement levels at all centres within 2 weeks—prioritise sites with low booking rates or recent complaints about energy/enthusiasm.

• Implement mandatory coach transition protocols: notify centres 4+ weeks before any coach change and confirm replacement coach meets centre standards before transition occurs.

• Schedule weekly check-ins with centres during first month of new coach assignment to catch dissatisfaction early and address concerns before cancellation.

• Review equipment and program difficulty at at-risk centres—gather parent feedback on challenge level and ensure resources match centre expectations before contract renewal dates.

patterns.prevention:
• Contact centres within 2 weeks of sign-up to confirm program timing works for their schedule and identify any conflicts early.

• Schedule quarterly check-ins with all active centres to gather feedback, address concerns, and discuss their internal capacity before they decide to switch providers.

• Develop a tiered pricing or package option for centres looking to transition services in-house, positioning us as a hybrid/supplementary partner rather than a full replacement.

• Brief coaches monthly on cancellation trends and equip them to flag centres showing disengagement during sessions so managers can intervene proactively.

Affinity  Group -direction to cut all incursions immediately at center as already over budget. waived 4 session cancellation to assist with confirmation in writing we will be preferred provider as soon as incursions are back email confirmation

Date Started: 28/11/2025
Number of Sessions Completed: 22
Sessions Missed: 0
Rescheduled: 0

Coaches: Mataus ,Patsy

Summary of their time with us: 
The first week of sessions was described as going well, with the coach, Mateu  noted for being polite, professional, and on time, and there were no concerns raised at this early stage. As the program moved into December 2025, feedback remained stable and positive, with no additional bookings requested at that point but still no issues identified, reinforcing that the program had been well received from the outset.

In January 2026, minor operational adjustments were made to better suit the centre’s schedule, including trialling session times between 1:00 pm and 2:30 pm, which were later confirmed as workable. This period reflected a practical alignment phase where the service was being fine-tuned to fit the centre’s routine while maintaining strong satisfaction. By February 2026, direct feedback from the centre continued to be highly positive, with Mateus again described as “great” and “amazing,” consistently on time, and with no complaints raised, indicating growing confidence in the program’s reliability.

In March 2026, further internal coordination took place, including a confirmed adjustment to a 2:00 pm start time, showing continued responsiveness to centre needs. Around the same time, broader feedback highlighted very strong satisfaction, with both coaches Mateus Gomes and Patricia (Patsy) Del Valle being praised for their energy, engagement, and ability to connect with children. Staff noted that the children were actively enjoying the sessions, and the coaches were described as having positive, bubbly personalities and being highly attentive and professional.

By April 2026, the program had fully stabilised and was operating at a consistently high level, with explicit feedback confirming that Mateus was engaging well with children and delivering sessions that were both enjoyable and well-received. Staff reiterated that children loved the activities and that the coach was a strong positive influence in the program, with no concerns raised at this stage.

In May 2026, feedback continued to reflect satisfaction with the program, with the centre confirming they were happy with the sessions and the coach, although Aletia was noted as being away at the time. Shortly after, the centre made the decision to cancel the program due to budget constraints rather than any issues with delivery or satisfaction. The final communication acknowledged that the service had been well liked and that both the coach and sessions had been appreciated, with an indication that they may reconnect in future when budget allows.

Overall, the timeline reflects a consistently positive service experience from start to finish, characterised by strong coach performance, high child engagement, smooth operational adjustments, and stable satisfaction, with the only reason for discontinuation being external financial constraints rather than program quality or delivery issues.

Next point of contact: purely budget happy to have us back asap when they have budget

summary of time with us
Dec 2025 2025, with initial feedback during November and December indicating that children were engaged and enjoying the sessions. Some early developmental feedback was provided for Coach Mateus, including increasing engagement and energy levels with older children, improving time management across Kindy groups, and creating clearer progression between activities. Following the Christmas period, sessions resumed in January 2026, with final schedule details confirmed on 20 January. On 28 January 2026, more detailed feedback was received from the centre regarding Coach Mateus, with educators noting that while he was well-liked and engaged positively with children, sessions appeared repetitive, lacked visible progression between age groups, and required stronger structure and preparation. Mini Monstars responded immediately by implementing a support plan, arranging for Coach Patsy to shadow sessions and preparing for a coaching transition. However, Patsy's unexpected unavailability resulted in further disruption, and on 11 February 2026 the centre raised concerns after Mateus attended alone when a transition had been expected. Mini Monstars acted quickly and permanently reassigned the service to Coach Aisa, communicating the change and providing reassurance to the centre. The impact was immediate, with feedback on 24 February 2026 describing Aisa as "amazing, incredible and perfect," and on 20 April 2026 Melanie confirmed that the children loved the sessions, had no complaints, and that Aisa was doing a fantastic job. During this period, Mini Monstars also worked closely with the centre regarding Affinity invoicing processes and payment procedures, helping navigate several administrative challenges between February and April. On 30 April 2026, after the centre advised they were experiencing budget pressures, Mini Monstars proposed a significantly reduced session structure and discounted pricing model in an attempt to retain services while reducing costs. Despite these efforts, Melanie advised on 12 and 13 May 2026 that the centre was unable to continue due to budget limitations. She reiterated that the decision was not related to program quality, expressed how much the centre loved Coach Aisa and the sessions, and advised they would like Mini Monstars to return as soon as budgets allowed in the new financial year. Following cancellation, Mini Monstars continued supporting the centre with invoice clarification requests throughout June 2026. Overall, Papilio Early Learning Robina's journey included an initially challenging coaching period that was successfully resolved through decisive action and a coach change, resulting in a highly positive experience under Coach Aisa. The centre's eventual cancellation was driven by budget constraints rather than dissatisfaction with the program, and the relationship concluded with a strong opportunity for future re-engagement
